content.full_text:
3807 Robie Lee Way
Sacramento, CA 95821-3246
December 24, 1991


John Wallace, Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

      RE:  Request for Advice Relative to the Application of Article 1 of Chapter 9.5
           of Title 9 of the Government Code

Dear Mr. Wallace:

     This letter is to request written advice relative to the acceptance of an honorarium
for a speech given on December 9, 1991, at a private conference in Maui, Hawaii. This
advice is being requested pursuant to Government Code section 83114, subdivision (b)¹
and Title 2, California Code of Regulations, Section 18329(b).

                                    BACKGROUND

     I am a designated employee in the Office of the Secretary of State, Elections
Division. My permanent civil service classification is Senior Staff Counsel. The Secretary
of State has adopted a Conflict of Interest Code. With respect to my disclosure category
under this Code, my personnel officer has notified me that I am required to disclose on
my statement of economic interest the following:

     (1)   Any investment in, income from, or business position with any business
           entity which produces, markets or distributes voting machines, or vote
           tabulating devices, or which produces, markets, or distributes paper or
           paper products.


     ___________________________
     ¹All statutory references are to the California Government Code unless stated
otherwise.

John Wallace
December 24, 1991
Page Two


(2)     Any income received directly or indirectly from any lobbyist, employer of a
lobbyist, candidate for state office, elected state official, political party
official, or person holding a business position with any campaign
committee.

(3)     Any investment in, income from, or business position with any business
entity which manufactures, markets, distributes or leases electronic data
processing equipment, forms, supplies, or services.


The Pacific Conference was held in Maui, Hawaii during the week of December 8,
1991 to December 13, 1991. The sponsoring organization is a private nonprofit
corporation whose stated purpose is to "promote interstate and international cooperation
in the Pacific Region." (See photocopy of the Pacific Conference informational brochure
attached hereto as Exhibit 1.) I was asked to give a speech on the topic of Proposition
140, the term limitation initiative appearing on the November 1990 General Election
ballot, and the recent California Supreme Court decision addressing the constitutionality
of California's term limit law. (See 1991 Conference agenda attached hereto as Exhibit
2.) For this speech I was offered a $1,000 honorarium plus lodging and subsistence.
Travel was paid from my personal assets.

RELEVANT LAW

Section 89500 et seq. addresses acceptance of honoraria, gifts, and payments for
travel by public officials and employees. Specifically, prohibitions against accepting
honoraria are found at subdivision (a) of Section 89503. This section prohibits a state
employee from accepting an honorarium if the employee "would be required to report
the receipt of income or gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic
interest."

John Wallace
December 24, 1991
Page Three


QUESTION

May I accept the $1,000 honorarium and lodging and subsistence from the Pacific
Conference? If the answer to this question is in the negative, what expenses, if any,
incurred during this conference are reimbursable by the Pacific Conference?

Thank you for your attention to this matter. Should you need additional
information or if you have any questions, please contact me at 445-0820.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Richard Nishite


Attachments
